steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The verb is “eschew,” followed by the object “currency” and then the prepositional phrase “in favor of a direct trade system.” The relative clause “that requires...” follows this phrase.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Commas should not separate a verb from an essential (non-parenthetical) prepositional phrase that modifies it. Choice B correctly avoids punctuation before “in favor of a direct trade system,” maintaining a clear structure where “that requires...” unambiguously modifies “system.” Choices A, C, and D introduce commas or dashes that violate this rule by either separating the verb from its essential modifier or creating ambiguity about what the relative clause modifies. That matches choice B — B is correct.
